hound_testkit0.1.0

In crate hound_testkit

hound_testkit

Module env 

Source
Expand description

Env: the shared per-process scenario harness — backend/Mailpit endpoints, a lazily-bootstrapped default community (general/voice space pair), and per-run-unique participant emails.

The bootstrap (admin sign-up → community create → instance provision) is a Rust port of what crates/bot/e2e.sh used to do by hand with curl/jq; the shell script now owns only the compose stack (see its module doc).

Bootstrap under nextest’s process-per-test model: every test gets its own process, so “exactly once per process” (the static OnceCell below) mostly just protects a single non-nextest cargo test binary running many #[tokio::test]s. Across processes sharing one backend, only the literal first-ever registrant holds ManageCommunities (services/backend/src/api/community.rs) — so at most one racing process can actually create+provision the shared community; every other process’s bootstrap polls GET /instance until that lands. See bootstrap.

Structs§

Bootstrap 🔒
Env
The shared scenario harness for one test process. Cheap to clone.
Inner 🔒
InstanceModel 🔒

Constants§

PROVISION_POLL_TIMEOUT 🔒
How long a losing bootstrap process waits for the winner to provision the instance before giving up.

Statics§

BOOTSTRAP 🔒

Functions§

admin_sign_up 🔒
Sign up a throwaway bootstrap account (per-call-unique email built from prefix) over raw REST, returning its access token. Mirrors hound-testkit’s own auth::sign_in_magic_link, but returns the bearer token directly (needed for the admin-only /community and /instance calls below, which have no typed hound-client surface). Magic start → Mailpit code → verify → register, once through — the backend’s first-registration path is race-safe (services/backend/src/api/auth.rs), so concurrent bootstrap processes registering at the same time never need a retry here.
bootstrap 🔒
Bootstrap the shared community. See the module doc for the cross-process race handling.
fetch_voice_and_text_spaces 🔒
Find the general(text)/voice pair among community_id’s spaces — auto-created alongside every community (services/backend/src/api/community.rs::provision_default_layout).
get_instance 🔒
init_test_tracing 🔒
Install a stderr tracing subscriber for the test process so hound-client call-plane warnings (control WS drops, reconnect attempts) land in nextest’s captured per-test output — the difference between a diagnosable e2e failure and a bare timeout. Idempotent; RUST_LOG overrides the default filter.
resolve_as_member 🔒
Resolve the provisioned default community’s space pair with a guaranteed-member token: a fresh account registered now (i.e. strictly post-provisioning) auto-joins the default community — necessary because space listing is membership-gated (filter_accessible_spaces — a non-member sees an empty page, not an error), and whether our own bootstrap account auto-joined depends on registration/provision ordering.
settled_default_community 🔒
Poll /instance until provisioned, returning default_community. Bounded by PROVISION_POLL_TIMEOUT — this waits out a genuine cross-process race (some other test process is still mid-bootstrap), not a workaround: the backend’s provision write is atomic, so the first provisioned=true read already carries the settled value.
try_create_community 🔒
POST /community — requires ManageCommunities, which only the literal first-ever registrant on this DB holds. Ok(None) on a 403 (we’re not that account); other non-success statuses are hard errors.
try_provision 🔒
POST /instance/provision. Ok(true) on success, Ok(false) on a 409 (already provisioned — a race we lost).
